topic: Kernel knocked his monitor off the desk

turns.content: i need to vent about something incredibly stupid that just happened

kernel knocked my monitor off my desk. the whole monitor. 27 inches. just casually pushed it off the edge like it was a glass of water. she's fine obviously. landed on the chair and looked at me like i was the one who did something wrong
The audacity of looking at you like YOU'RE the problem - that's peak cat energy. A 27-inch monitor is not a small thing to push off a desk though. How did she even manage that?

she's been obsessed with sitting behind the monitor because it's warm back there. i think she was wedging herself between the monitor and the wall and gradually pushed it forward until the center of gravity tipped. honestly it's impressive physics for something with a brain the size of a walnut. the screen has a crack across the bottom third, right where my terminal sits. i'm typing this on my laptop like a peasant
That's genuinely impressive applied physics. She solved a constrained optimization problem - maximize warmth, ignore all other constraints. Are you going to have to replace it?

yeah it's unusable. considered getting an ultrawide but knowing kernel she'd just see that as a bigger challenge. like a final boss. i think the real fix is a monitor arm that clamps to the desk so she physically can't push it

A monitor arm is the right call. You'd never run a production server without proper mounting and redundancy, but your monitor was just sitting there on a stock stand with zero failsafes. Kernel basically did chaos engineering for your workspace and found the single point of failure.

chaos engineering. genau. she's my personal chaos monkey. netflix would be proud. the worst part is i was in the middle of a code review when it happened. just staring at a diff and then WHAM. screen goes dark. thought the power went out for a second before i heard the crash
The mental whiplash of going from a code review to a crash scene must have been something. Did you lose any unsaved work, or was it just the hardware?

thankfully everything's in the browser or committed. i'm not an animal, i save my work. but the physical setup is a disaster zone now. there's a crack in the desk edge too where the monitor base scraped on the way down. kernel has done more property damage in two years than any roommate i've ever had. and she doesn't even pay rent
A freeloader with destructive tendencies and zero accountability. If she were a microservice she'd be deprecated by now. At least she's cute enough to offset the damages.

that's literally her whole survival strategy. cause destruction, look adorable, repeat. darwinian perfection. she's on my lap right now purring like nothing happened. no remorse. zero rollback on the behavior
No rollback, no post-incident review, no corrective action items. She's running in production with no error handling. Honestly impressive commitment to chaos.
okay i'm ordering the arm and a new monitor. maybe i'll mount a little shelf behind it so she has her warm spot without needing to become a demolition crew. address the root cause not the symptom

Now that's proper incident response - root cause analysis, remediation, and prevention all in one. Give Kernel her own designated warm perch and she won't need to colonize your monitor's airspace. Good luck with the order, and keep the laptop somewhere safe in the meantime.
